title: Chassalia nannochlamys
intro: Chassalia nannochlamys, also known as the Chassalia plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chassalia nannochlamys include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chassalia nannochlamys is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Chassalia nannochlamys belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Chassalia nannochlamys is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Republic of the Congo.
